_**User (2026-02-24 06:54:28Z)**_

Implement the following plan:

# Action-Oriented Tasks Tab for Sales Pipeline

## Context

The sales pipeline currently has 3 tabs (Outreach, Pipeline, Orbit) that show data by stage. Inspired by the HoloHive CRM demo, we want to add a **task-oriented "Actions" tab** as the primary workflow view. Instead of "here are your leads by stage", it answers "what do I need to do right now?" with one-click execution.

## What Changes

**Single file**: `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx` (no new files, no service changes)

### 1. `getNextAction()` helper function

Pure function that takes an opportunity and returns what to do next:

| Stage | Condition | Action Label | Priority |
|---|---|---|---|
| Any post-discovery | BAMFAM violation (no future meeting) | "Book Next Meeting!" | urgent |
| `v2_contract` | — | "Get Signed" | urgent |
| `proposal_call` | — | "Close Deal" | urgent |
| `cold_dm` | bump_number = 0 | "Send First DM" | high |
| `cold_dm` | last_bump >= 3 days ago | "Bump #N" | high |
| `cold_dm` | last_bump < 3 days | "Wait Xd" | wait (filtered out) |
| `cold_dm` | bump_number >= 4 | "Review → Orbit" | medium |
| `warm` | interested | "Get TG Handle" | high |
| `warm` | silent | "Follow Up" | high |
| `tg_intro` | — | "Book Call" | high |
| `booked` | no next_meeting | "Schedule Meeting" | high |
| `booked` | meeting scheduled | "Prep for Meeting" | low |
| `discovery_done` | no proposal_sent_at | "Send Proposal" | high |
| `proposal_sent` | — | "Follow Up Proposal" | high |
| `orbit` | created > 90 days ago | "Resurrect Check" | low |

Returns: `{ label, priority, actionType: 'bump' | 'stage_change' | 'open_detail' | 'none', targetStage?, isActionable, sortScore }`

### 2. `actionItems` useMemo

Derives from existing `opportunities` state (already has all opps loaded):
- Maps each opp through `getNextAction()`
- Filters by `actionFilter` state: "all" / "mine" / "urgent"
- Filters out non-actionable items (wait state) and terminal stages
- Sorts by `sortScore` ascending (most urgent first), then by temperature descending

### 3. `handleActionExecute()` handler

One-click dispatcher that reuses existing handlers:
- `actionType: 'bump'` → calls existing `handleRecordBump()`
- `actionType: 'stage_change'` → calls existing `handleStageChange()` (orbit/closed_lost prompts still work)
- `actionType: 'open_detail'` → opens existing slide-over panel

### 4. New state (2 variables only)

```typescript
const [actionFilter, setActionFilter] = useState<'all' | 'mine' | 'urgent'>('mine');
const [executingAction, setExecutingAction] = useState<string | null>(null);
```

### 5. Tab changes

- Change `activeTab` type to include `'actions'` and default to `'actions'`
- Add "Actions" as the **first** tab with a Zap icon and action count badge
- Add `renderActionsTab()` function

### 6. `renderActionsTab()` UI

- **Filter sub-tabs**: All Actions / My Actions / Urgent Only (pill buttons, same style as outreach owner sub-tabs)
- **Table** with columns: Name, Stage, Bucket, Next Action (with priority icon), Days Silent, Temp, Owner, Execute button
- Urgent rows get subtle red background tint
- Execute button style varies by priority (destructive for urgent, default for high, outline for low)
- Row click opens slide-over detail
- Empty state: "No actions needed right now" message
- No new imports needed — all icons and components already imported

## Verification

1. `npm run build` passes
2. Actions tab loads as default, shows prioritized list
3. "My Actions" filter shows only current user's opps
4. "Urgent Only" shows BAMFAM violations + close-stage items
5. Clicking "Bump #N" button executes bump and item moves to wait state (disappears)
6. Clicking "Review → Orbit" opens orbit reason dialog
7. Clicking "Get TG Handle" / "Schedule Meeting" opens slide-over
8. Stage change buttons advance the opp correctly

```text
   219→    const pastColdDm = all.filter(o => o.stage !== 'cold_dm');
   220→    const coldDmCount = outreachTotal > 0 ? outreachTotal : all.filter(o => o.stage === 'cold_dm').length;
   221→    const totalDmsSent = all.length; // every opp started as a DM
   222→    const responseRate = totalDmsSent > 0 ? (pastColdDm.length / totalDmsSent) * 100 : 0;
   223→
   224→    // Meetings booked = in booked or any stage after it
   225→    const bookedAndBeyond = ['booked', 'discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract', 'v2_closed_won'];
   226→    const meetingsBooked = all.filter(o => bookedAndBeyond.includes(o.stage)).length;
   227→
   228→    // Discovery calls = in discovery_done or later
   229→    const discoveryAndBeyond = ['discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract', 'v2_closed_won'];
   230→    const discoveryCalls = all.filter(o => discoveryAndBeyond.includes(o.stage)).length;
   231→
   232→    // Close rate = won / (won + lost)
   233→    const totalClosed = closedWon.length + closedLost.length;
   234→    const closeRate = totalClosed > 0 ? (closedWon.length / totalClosed) * 100 : 0;
   235→
   236→    // Avg deal size (closed won)
   237→    const wonValues = closedWon.map(o => o.deal_value || 0).filter(v => v > 0);
   238→    const avgDealSize = wonValues.length > 0 ? wonValues.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / wonValues.length : 0;
   239→
   240→    // Avg close time (days from created_at to closed_at for won deals)
   241→    const closeTimes = closedWon
   242→      .filter(o => o.created_at && o.closed_at)
   243→      .map(o => (new Date(o.closed_at!).getTime() - new Date(o.created_at).getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
   244→    const avgCloseTime = closeTimes.length > 0 ? closeTimes.reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / closeTimes.length : 0;
   245→
   246→    // Qualified % = bucket A + B / total
   247→    const qualifiedCount = all.filter(o => o.bucket === 'A' || o.bucket === 'B').length;
   248→    const qualifiedPct = all.length > 0 ? (qualifiedCount / all.length) * 100 : 0;
   249→
   250→    // Bucket A %
   251→    const bucketAPct = all.length > 0 ? (all.filter(o => o.bucket === 'A').length / all.length) * 100 : 0;
   252→
   253→    // Pipeline value (active)
   254→    const pipelineValue = pipelineActive.reduce((sum, o) => sum + (o.deal_value || 0), 0);
   255→
   256→    // Overdue follow-ups

   258→    const overdueFollowups = pipelineActive.filter(o =>

   261→

   267→    return {
```

```text
   482→  const handleRecordBump = async (oppId: string) => {
   483→    if (isBumping) return;

   486→    setOpportunities(prev => prev.map(o =>

   488→    ));
   489→    try {
   490→      await SalesPipelineService.recordBump(oppId);

   498→      await fetchData(); // revert on error
   499→    } finally {
   500→      setIsBumping(false);
   501→    }
   502→  };

   509→      o.id === oppId ? { ...o, bump_number: Math.max(o.bump_number - 1, 0) } : o
   510→    ));
   511→    try {
   512→      await SalesPipelineService.reduceBump(oppId);
   513→      await fetchData();
   514→    } catch (err) {
   515→      console.error('Error reducing bump:', err);
   516→      await fetchData(); // revert on error
   517→    } finally {

   526→      await SalesPipelineService.createActivity({
   527→        ...activityForm,
   528→        opportunity_id: slideOverOpp.id,

   530→      setActivityForm({ opportunity_id: '', type: 'note', title: '' });

   541→      const updates: any = {};

   543→        updates.deal_value = value ? parseFloat(value) : null;
   544→      } else if (field === 'temperature_score') {

   576→    setActiveId(id);
   577→    setActiveOpportunity(opportunities.find(o => o.id === id) || null);
   578→  };
   579→
   580→  const handleDragEnd = async (event: DragEndEvent) => {
   581→    const { active, over } = event;
   582→    setActiveId(null);
   583→    setActiveOpportunity(null);
   584→
   585→    if (!over) return;
   586→
   587→    const oppId = active.id as string;
   588→    const overId = over.id as string;
   589→    const opp = opportunities.find(o => o.id === oppId);
   590→    if (!opp) return;
   591→
   592→    // Check if dropped on a stage column
   593→    const isStageColumn = ALL_V2_STAGES.includes(overId as SalesPipelineStage);
   594→
   595→    if (isStageColumn) {
   596→      const newStage = overId as SalesPipelineStage;
   597→      if (opp.stage !== newStage) {
   598→        await handleStageChange(oppId, newStage, opp.stage);
   599→      }
```

```text
   740→                {opp.dm_account === 'sdr' && (
```

```text
   895→                        No opportunities
   896→                      </div>
   897→                    ) : (
   898→                      stageOpps.map(opp => (
   899→                        <SortableCard key={opp.id} id={opp.id}>
   900→                          {renderCard(opp)}
   901→                        </SortableCard>
   902→                      ))
   903→                    )}
   904→                  </SortableContext>
   905→                </DroppableColumn>
   906→              )}
   907→            </div>
   908→          );
   909→        })}
   910→
   911→        {/* Orbit drop zone */}
   912→        <div className="w-12 flex flex-col h-full transition-all duration-200">
   913→          <DroppableColumn id="orbit" className="rounded-lg px-4 py-3 bg-orange-50 border border-orange-200 flex-shrink-0 cursor-pointer select-none">
   914→            <div className="flex flex-col items-center gap-1">
   915→              <RotateCcw className="w-4 h-4 text-orange-700" />
   916→              <span className="font-semibold text-orange-700 mt-2" style={{ writingMode: 'vertical-rl', textOrientation: 'mixed' }}>
   917→                Orbit
   918→              </span>
   919→              <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-xs font-medium mt-1">{orbitOpps.length}</Badge>
   920→            </div>
   921→          </DroppableColumn>
   922→        </div>
   923→
   924→        {/* Closed Lost drop zone */}
   925→        <div className="w-12 flex flex-col h-full transition-all duration-200">
   926→          <DroppableColumn id="v2_closed_lost" className="rounded-lg px-4 py-3 bg-red-50 border border-red-200 flex-shrink-0 cursor-pointer select-none">
   927→            <div className="flex flex-col items-center gap-1">
   928→              <X className="w-4 h-4 text-red-700" />
   929→              <span className="font-semibold text-red-700 mt-2" style={{ writingMode: 'vertical-rl', textOrientation: 'mixed' }}>
   930→                Lost
   931→              </span>
   932→              <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-xs font-medium mt-1">
   933→                {filteredOpportunities.filter(o => o.stage === 'v2_closed_lost').length}
   934→              </Badge>
   935→            </div>
   936→          </DroppableColumn>
   937→        </div>
   938→      </div>
   939→
   940→      <DragOverlay>
   941→        {activeOpportunity ? (
   942→          <div className="w-[280px]">
   943→            {renderCard(activeOpportunity, true)}
   944→          </div>
   945→        ) : null}
   946→      </DragOverlay>
   947→    </DndContext>
   948→  );
   949→
   950→  // ============================================
   951→  // RENDER: Table View
   952→  // ============================================
   953→
   954→  const renderTable = () => (
   955→    <div className="pb-8">
   956→      <DndContext sensors={sensors} collisionDetection={closestCenter} onDragStart={handleDragStart} onDragEnd={handleDragEnd}>
   957→        {visiblePipelineStages.map(stage => {
   958→          const stageOpps = getStageOpps(stage);
   959→          const colors = STAGE_COLORS[stage];
   960→          const isCollapsed = collapsedStages.has(stage);
   961→          const stageValue = stageOpps.reduce((s, o) => s + (o.deal_value || 0), 0);
   962→
   963→          return (
   964→            <div key={stage} className="mb-6">
   965→              {/* Stage Header */}
   966→              <div
   967→                onClick={() => {
   968→                  const next = new Set(collapsedStages);
   969→                  isCollapsed ? next.delete(stage) : next.add(stage);
   970→                  setCollapsedStages(next);
   971→                }}
   972→                className={`flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 ${colors.bg} ${isCollapsed ? 'rounded-lg' : 'rounded-t-lg'} border ${colors.border} ${isCollapsed ? '' : 'border-b-0'} cursor-pointer select-none transition-all`}
   973→              >
   974→                <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
   975→                  {isCollapsed ? <ChevronRight className={`h-4 w-4 ${colors.text}`} /> : <ChevronDown className={`h-4 w-4 ${colors.text}`} />}
   976→                  <h4 className={`font-semibold ${colors.text}`}>{STAGE_LABELS[stage]}</h4>
   977→                  <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-xs font-medium">{stageOpps.length}</Badge>
   978→                </div>
   979→                {stageValue > 0 && (
   980→                  <span className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-600">
   981→                    ${stageValue.toLocaleString()}
   982→                  </span>
   983→                )}
   984→              </div>
   985→
   986→              {!isCollapsed && (
   987→                <div className="bg-white rounded-b-lg border border-gray-200 border-t-0">
   988→                  <Table>
   989→                    <TableHeader>
   990→                      <TableRow className="bg-gray-50/50">
   991→                        <TableHead className="w-10"></TableHead>
   992→                        <TableHead>Name</TableHead>
   993→                        <TableHead className="w-[70px]">Bucket</TableHead>
   994→                        <TableHead className="w-[70px]">DM</TableHead>
   995→                        <TableHead className="w-[90px]">Temp</TableHead>
   996→                        <TableHead className="w-[110px]">Value</TableHead>
   997→                        <TableHead className="w-[100px]">Owner</TableHead>
   998→                        <TableHead className="w-[100px]">TG Handle</TableHead>
   999→                        {stage === 'cold_dm' && <TableHead className="w-[80px]">Bumps</TableHead>}
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 06:57:28Z)**_

Done. Here's what was implemented in `app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`:

1. **`getNextAction()` helper** — Pure function mapping each opportunity to its next action based on stage, BAMFAM violations, bump status, days since last contact, etc. Returns label, priority, actionType, targetStage, and sortScore.

2. **`actionItems` useMemo** — Derives from `opportunities`, filters out terminal stages and non-actionable (wait) items, applies the `actionFilter` (all/mine/urgent), sorts by urgency then temperature.

3. **`handleActionExecute()` handler** — One-click dispatcher that routes to `handleRecordBump()` for bumps, `handleStageChange()` for stage changes (orbit dialog still triggers), or `openSlideOver()` for detail-oriented actions.

4. **New state** — `actionFilter` (defaults to `'mine'`) and `executingAction` (loading state per row).

5. **Tab changes** — `activeTab` now defaults to `'actions'`, Actions tab appears first with Zap icon and count badge.

6. **`renderActionsTab()` UI** — Filter pills (All/My/Urgent), table with Name, Stage, Bucket, Next Action (with priority icon), Days Silent, Temp, Owner, and Execute button. Urgent rows get red tint, button variants change by priority, empty state shows "No actions needed right now".

---

```text
  1709→  const renderActionsTab = () => {
  1710→    const getPriorityIcon = (priority: ActionPriority) => {
  1711→      switch (priority) {
  1712→        case 'urgent': return <AlertTriangle className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-red-500" />;
  1713→        case 'high': return <Zap className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-amber-500" />;
  1714→        case 'medium': return <Clock className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-blue-500" />;
  1715→        case 'low': return <Clock className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-400" />;
  1716→        default: return null;
  1717→      }
  1718→    };
  1719→
  1720→    const getButtonVariant = (priority: ActionPriority): 'destructive' | 'default' | 'outline' => {
  1721→      if (priority === 'urgent') return 'destructive';
  1722→      if (priority === 'high') return 'default';
  1723→      return 'outline';
  1724→    };
  1725→
  1726→    const getDaysSilent = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity) => {
  1727→      const lastDate = opp.last_contacted_at || opp.last_bump_date || opp.created_at;
  1728→      if (!lastDate) return null;
  1729→      return Math.floor((Date.now() - new Date(lastDate).getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
  1730→    };
  1731→
  1732→    return (
  1733→      <div className="pb-8">
  1734→        {/* Filter sub-tabs */}
  1735→        <div className="flex items-center gap-1 mb-4">
  1736→          {([
  1737→            { key: 'all' as const, label: 'All Actions' },
  1738→            { key: 'mine' as const, label: 'My Actions' },
  1739→            { key: 'urgent' as const, label: 'Urgent Only' },
  1740→          ]).map(f => (
  1741→            <button
  1742→              key={f.key}
  1743→              onClick={() => setActionFilter(f.key)}
  1744→              className={`px-3 py-1.5 text-xs font-medium rounded-md transition-colors ${
  1745→                actionFilter === f.key
  1746→                  ? 'bg-sky-100 text-sky-700 border border-sky-200'
  1747→                  : 'text-gray-600 hover:bg-gray-100 border border-transparent'
  1748→              }`}
  1749→            >
  1750→              {f.label}
  1751→            </button>
  1752→          ))}
  1753→        </div>
  1754→
  1755→        {/* Table */}
  1756→        <div className="bg-white rounded-lg border border-gray-200">
  1757→          <Table>
  1758→            <TableHeader>
  1759→              <TableRow className="bg-gray-50/50">
  1760→                <TableHead>Name</TableHead>
  1761→                <TableHead className="w-[110px]">Stage</TableHead>
  1762→                <TableHead className="w-[70px]">Bucket</TableHead>
  1763→                <TableHead className="w-[180px]">Next Action</TableHead>
  1764→                <TableHead className="w-[80px]">Days Silent</TableHead>
  1765→                <TableHead className="w-[90px]">Temp</TableHead>
  1766→                <TableHead className="w-[100px]">Owner</TableHead>
  1767→                <TableHead className="w-[120px]"></TableHead>
  1768→              </TableRow>
  1769→            </TableHeader>
  1770→            <TableBody>
  1771→              {actionItems.length === 0 ? (
  1772→                <TableRow>
  1773→                  <TableCell colSpan={8} className="text-center py-12">
  1774→                    <div className="flex flex-col items-center gap-2 text-gray-400">
  1775→                      <Zap className="h-8 w-8" />
  1776→                      <p className="text-sm font-medium">No actions needed right now</p>
  1777→                      <p className="text-xs">All caught up — check back later</p>
  1778→                    </div>
  1779→                  </TableCell>
  1780→                </TableRow>
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 07:17:12Z)**_

I found a bug. Let me check the BAMFAM logic more carefully:

The plan says for **any post-discovery stage** with a BAMFAM violation, return "Book Next Meeting!" as urgent. But the BAMFAM check runs first, *before* the switch. So if a `v2_contract` opp has a BAMFAM violation, it returns "Book Next Meeting!" instead of "Get Signed". And if a `proposal_call` opp has BAMFAM, same thing. That's actually **correct per the plan** — BAMFAM overrides everything.

But there's a subtler issue: `v2_contract` and `proposal_call` are in `postDiscovery`, so if they *do* have a future meeting, they fall through to the switch and get their own action. If they *don't*, they get BAMFAM. That's fine.

Now let me check a logic issue: `v2_contract` with a future meeting will hit the BAMFAM check (`hasFutureMeeting = true`), skip it, then hit the switch case and return "Get Signed" as urgent. But per the plan, "Get Signed" should only show when there's no BAMFAM violation — otherwise BAMFAM takes priority. Actually wait, re-reading: the switch case for `v2_contract` always returns "Get Signed" regardless. That means if you have a future meeting on a `v2_contract`, you get "Get Signed" — which is correct. The BAMFAM only fires when there's no future meeting.

One real issue I see: **the `actionItems` useMemo calls `getNextAction` twice** for the urgent filter — once in the `.map()` and once in the second `.filter()`. The second call recalculates redundantly when it already has `action` available.

Build passes. Here's what the "Timing" column now shows per context:

| Stage/Situation | Shows |
|---|---|

| Cold DM, bumped | "Bumped 5d ago" (amber if >= 3d) |
| Has next_meeting in future | "Meeting in 2d" (blue if <= 2d) |
| Has next_meeting in past | "Meeting 3d ago" (red) |
| Meeting today | "Meeting today" (blue) |
| Proposal sent | "Sent 5d ago" (amber if >= 5d) |
| Orbit | "45d in orbit" (amber if > 90d) |
| Everything else | "7d silent" (red >= 7d, amber >= 3d) |

| Action | Hint |

| Review → Orbit | 4 bumps sent, no reply — shelve or orbit |
| Get TG Handle | They replied — ask for Telegram to continue there |
| Follow Up | No reply yet — send another message on DM |
| Book Call | Send Calendly link on TG to schedule |
| Schedule Meeting | Set the meeting date & time |
| Prep for Meeting | Review notes & prep talking points |
| Send Proposal | Draft & send pricing proposal |
| Follow Up Proposal | Check if they reviewed the proposal |
| Resurrect Check | 90+ days — re-engage or mark lost |

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 14:30:09Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user is pointing out that the action buttons for "Get Signed" and "Close Deal" currently only have one option (advance to next stage), but in reality the outcome might not be positive - the deal might not close, they might need to follow up more, or it could go to orbit/lost. Also, since there are two paths (Path A closer, Path B SDR), the actions should reflect which path the opp is on.

The key insight is: the Actions tab should present **multiple outcome options** for each action, not just a single "advance" button. Like:
- "Get Signed" → options: "Signed!" (→ closed_won), "Follow Up" (open detail), "Lost" (→ closed_lost)


- "Close Deal" → options: "Moving to Contract" (→ v2_contract), "Follow Up" (open detail), "Lost" (→ closed_lost)
- "Book Call" → options: "Booked!" (→ booked), "No Response" (open detail), "Orbit" (→ orbit)

So instead of a single execute button that assumes success, I need to show a set of small buttons per action representing the different possible outcomes. The primary happy path should stand out visually, with secondary options like follow-up or orbit tucked alongside.

Looking back at what they actually asked for—they want fallback options when things don't go as planned, and they want the two different paths (closer vs SDR) reflected in the interface. The action buttons are really about guiding operators through their workflow, while the pipeline and outreach views give managers visibility into what's happening.

So for each action type, I need to map out the realistic outcomes. For a cold DM bump, that might be "Bump" to record the bump, or "Replied!" to move it to warm conversations.  For warm follow-ups, the operator could mark "Got TG" to move to intro stage, or "Follow Up" to stay in the current action. When there's a booked call, the outcomes shift to "Scheduled," "Reschedule," or "No Show" depending on what actually happened. The pattern is: primary action that moves things forward, secondary actions for staying in place or pivoting, and an "Orbit" option as a catch-all when things stall.

Now I'm mapping out the later stages—once a proposal goes out, the next moves are either "Call Set" to schedule a discussion, "Follow Up" to keep pushing, or "Lost" if it's not going anywhere. After that call, we're either moving to contract, following up again, or marking it lost. Once signed, the deal closes as won, but there's still a follow-up option or lost path if something falls through. For the BAMFAM track, booked calls go straight to orbit if they don't convert. And from orbit, we can either try to resurrect with a cold DM or accept it as lost.

This is getting unwieldy. I think the cleanest approach is to pair each main action with a small dropdown showing the alternative outcomes—so instead of one execute button, there's a primary button for the happy path and a compact dropdown for the other options. Let me start building this with an alternatives array attached to each action, then render them as a dropdown menu next to the main button.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 14:31:50Z)**_

Build passes. Here's what changed:

**Every action now has a primary button + dropdown with alternative outcomes:**

| Stage | Primary Button | Dropdown Alternatives |
|---|---|---|
| Cold DM (bump) | `Bump #N` / `Send First DM` | Replied! (→ warm), Orbit |
| Cold DM (4+ bumps) | `Review → Orbit` | Replied! (→ warm), Keep Bumping |
| Warm (interested) | `Got TG!` (→ tg_intro) | Follow Up, Orbit |
| Warm (silent) | `Follow Up` | Got TG! (→ tg_intro), Orbit |
| TG Intro | `Booked!` (→ booked) | Follow Up, Orbit |
| Booked (no meeting) | `Schedule Meeting` | No Show, Orbit |
| Booked (has meeting) | `Discovery Done` (→ discovery_done) | Reschedule, No Show |
| Discovery Done (no proposal) | `Sent Proposal` (→ proposal_sent) | Need More Info, Lost |
| Discovery Done (has proposal) | `Follow Up Proposal` | Call Set (→ proposal_call), Lost |
| Proposal Sent | `Follow Up Proposal` | Call Set (→ proposal_call), Lost |
| Proposal Call | `To Contract` (→ v2_contract) | Follow Up, Lost |
| Contract | `Signed!` (→ closed_won) | Follow Up, Lost |
| BAMFAM violation | `Book Next Meeting!` | Orbit, Lost |
| Orbit (90+ days) | `Resurrect` | Lost |

Key changes to the primary labels too — renamed to reflect **outcomes** ("Got TG!", "Booked!", "Signed!", "Sent Proposal", "Discovery Done") instead of instructions ("Get TG Handle", "Book Call", "Get Signed"). The hint underneath still tells them *how*, but the button says *what happened*. This also fixed the warm/interested path — primary button now advances to `tg_intro` instead of just opening the slide-over.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 16:10:32Z)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Let me think through the logic carefully and find potential issues.

1. **"Booked" stage with meeting → "Discovery Done" button**: The primary button is "Discovery Done" which advances to `discovery_done` via `stage_change`. But what if the meeting hasn't happened yet? If someone has a meeting scheduled for next week, the button says "Discovery Done" — that's misleading. They shouldn't mark discovery as done before the meeting happens.

2. **"Got TG!" for warm/interested**: The primary button moves to `tg_intro` immediately. But what if they're interested but haven't given TG yet? The label implies you already got it, but the hint says "ask for Telegram." The button advances the stage before the user actually has the TG handle.


3. **"Signed!" for v2_contract**: Same problem — the button jumps straight to closed_won, but what if they're still waiting for the signature? The "Follow Up" option is buried in the dropdown while the main action assumes the deal is done.

4. **"To Contract" for proposal_call**: This advances to v2_contract, but there's no check for whether the call actually happened or if another conversation is needed first.

5. **BAMFAM check for discovery_done**: The logic fires before checking for proposals — if someone's in discovery_done with nothing sent and no upcoming meeting scheduled, it returns "Book Next Meeting!" instead of prompting to send a proposal first.

6. **Cold DM edge case**: If bump_number gets reset to 0 but last_bump_date still exists, it shows "Send First DM" even though they've already been contacted.

7. **"Discovery Done" button timing issue**: When someone has a booked meeting coming up, the button says "Discovery Done" which would skip past the meeting entirely — the action needs to change depending on whether that meeting is still in the future or already happened.

8. **Warm without sub_state**: When warm_sub_state is null instead of 'interested' or 'silent', the fallback logic doesn't have a clear action.

The button labels are actually outcome-recording, not action-performing — "Sent Proposal" means "I've sent it, mark it done," not "click here to send." That's the right mental model, but the core issue is that for booked leads with upcoming meetings, "Discovery Done" shouldn't be the primary action since discovery can't be marked complete before the meeting happens.

Looking at the code, it returns "Discovery Done" for all booked opportunities with a meeting date, regardless of timing. The original design had "Prep for Meeting" for scheduled future meetings, but I switched it to "Discovery Done" as the primary action — that's the mistake. For future meetings, the button should guide them to prep, not claim discovery is done. I also need to verify how the future meeting check works across different states like proposal_call.

I'm spotting three critical problems: booked opportunities with upcoming meetings show "Discovery Done" when they should show "Prep for Meeting," proposal_call with a future meeting incorrectly jumps to "To Contract" instead of prepping for the call, and v2_contract defaults to "Signed!" which assumes success too early when "Follow Up" or "Chase Signature" would be more realistic.

Beyond those, there are a few edge cases worth noting—the "Got TG!" button advances the stage even if the handle field is still empty, there's a bump reduction scenario that leaves cold DMs in an odd state, and BAMFAM is overriding the "Send Proposal" action in discovery_done when both are valid options. I'll focus on the critical ones when presenting this to the user.
</details></think>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 16:10:47Z)**_

Here's what I found:

---

**1. "Booked" with a future meeting → primary button is "Discovery Done" (stage_change)**
This is the biggest issue. If someone has a meeting scheduled for next Tuesday, the primary button says "Discovery Done" which would skip them past the meeting. It should show "Prep for Meeting" when the meeting is still in the future, and only show "Discovery Done" after the meeting date has passed.

**2. "Signed!" as the primary for v2_contract → immediately closes won**
One misclick marks the deal as closed won. That's a high-stakes accidental click. "Follow Up" or "Chase Signature" should probably be the primary, with "Signed!" as the positive quick alt.

**3. "Got TG!" for warm/interested → advances to tg_intro immediately**
The hint says "ask for Telegram" but the button advances the stage before the user has actually entered the TG handle. They'd move to tg_intro with an empty TG field.

**4. BAMFAM overrides "Send Proposal" in discovery_done**
If someone is in `discovery_done` with no proposal sent AND no future meeting, the BAMFAM check fires first and shows "Book Next Meeting!" — hiding the arguably more important "Send Proposal" action. Both matter, but the user never sees the proposal reminder.

**5. "No Show" in booked alternatives → goes to orbit**
The dropdown "No Show" does a `stage_change` to orbit, but a no-show isn't necessarily an orbit — they might just need rescheduling. It should probably open the slide-over so the user can decide, rather than auto-orbiting.

**6. Cold DM bump_number=0 with a last_bump_date (edge case)**
If bumps were manually reduced to 0, it shows "Send First DM" even though they've been contacted before. Minor but could confuse.

---

Issues 1-3 are the ones I'd fix. Want me to go ahead with those, or do you want to adjust the approach for any of them?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 16:15:15Z)**_

All 6 fixes applied and building clean. Here's the summary:

**Fix 1 — Booked with future meeting**: Now splits into two paths:
- Meeting is in the future → "Prep for Meeting" (open detail) with Reschedule/Orbit alts
- Meeting is in the past → "Discovery Done" (advance stage) with Reschedule/No Show alts

**Fix 2 — v2_contract too easy to misclick**: Primary is now "Chase Signature" (opens slide-over with guidance). "Signed!" is a visible green quick-alt button — deliberate, not accidental.

**Fix 3 — warm/interested skipped TG entry**: Primary is now "Get TG Handle" (opens slide-over with guidance to enter the handle). "Got TG!" is a visible quick-alt to advance only after they've entered it.

**Fix 4 — BAMFAM hiding Send Proposal**: For `discovery_done` with no proposal AND no meeting, the primary is now "Send Proposal" with the hint "Also book a follow-up meeting (BAMFAM)" — surfaces both actions.

**Fix 5 — No Show auto-orbiting**: "No Show" now opens the slide-over (with guidance) so the user can decide whether to reschedule or orbit, instead of automatically moving to orbit.

**Fix 6 — bump_number=0 with last_bump_date**: "Send First DM" now only shows when there's genuinely no `last_bump_date`. If bumps were reduced to 0 but there's history, it shows "Bump #1" instead.

---

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested implementation of an action-oriented "Actions" tab for the Sales Pipeline (`app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`). The tab should answer "what do I need to do right now?" with one-click execution, prioritized by urgency. Over multiple iterations, the user refined requirements to include: consistent UI, context-aware timing info, instructional hints per action, multiple outcome buttons (not just happy path), slide-over guidance banners, and fixes for 6 logic issues. Most recently, the user discovered that the edit form is missing a "Next Meeting" date field, which several guidance texts reference.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - React `useMemo` for deriving action items from opportunities
   - Pure function `getNextAction()` mapping opportunity state to prioritized actions
   - Action types: `bump`, `stage_change`, `open_detail`, `none`
   - Priority levels: `urgent`, `high`, `medium`, `low`, `wait`
   - `alternatives` array with `quick` flag for visible secondary buttons vs dropdown
   - `actionGuidance` state for contextual instructions in slide-over
   - BAMFAM (Book A Meeting From A Meeting) violation detection
   - Two sales paths: Closer (Path A) and SDR (Path B)
   - Supabase as database backend
   - Next.js with shadcn/ui components (Badge, Button, Table, DropdownMenu, etc.)
   - `SalesPipelineService` for CRUD operations
   - `CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData` form type extended with `next_meeting_at`

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`app/crm/sales-pipeline/page.tsx`** (sole file modified - ~2900+ lines)
     - This is the main sales pipeline page containing all tabs, state, handlers, and rendering
     - **New state variables** (after line ~200):
       ```typescript
       const [actionFilter, setActionFilter] = useState<'all' | 'mine' | 'urgent'>('mine');
       const [executingAction, setExecutingAction] = useState<string | null>(null);
       const [actionGuidance, setActionGuidance] = useState<{ label: string; hint: string } | null>(null);
       ```
     - **Form type extended**:
       ```typescript
       const [form, setForm] = useState<CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData & { next_meeting_at?: string }>({ name: '' });
       ```
     - **activeTab type changed** from `'outreach' | 'pipeline' | 'orbit'` to `'actions' | 'outreach' | 'pipeline' | 'orbit'` defaulting to `'actions'`
     - **Type definitions** (inside component):
       ```typescript
       type ActionPriority = 'urgent' | 'high' | 'medium' | 'low' | 'wait';
       type ActionExecutionType = 'bump' | 'stage_change' | 'open_detail' | 'none';
       type ActionAlternative = { label: string; actionType: ActionExecutionType; targetStage?: SalesPipelineStage; variant: 'default' | 'warn' | 'danger'; quick?: boolean };
       ```
     - **`getNextAction()` function** - Pure function returning `{ label, hint, priority, actionType, targetStage?, isActionable, sortScore, alternatives }`. Key logic after all fixes:
       - BAMFAM check fires first for post-discovery stages, BUT discovery_done without proposal shows "Send Proposal" instead (Fix #4)
       - v2_contract: "Chase Signature" (open_detail) primary, "Signed!" as quick alt (Fix #2)
       - proposal_call: "To Contract" (stage_change to v2_contract)
       - cold_dm: bump_number=0 WITHOUT last_bump_date → "Send First DM"; WITH last_bump_date → "Bump #1" (Fix #6)
       - cold_dm bumps: "Replied!" as quick alt moving to warm
       - warm/interested: "Get TG Handle" (open_detail) primary, "Got TG!" as quick alt (Fix #3)
       - booked: splits on future vs past meeting date (Fix #1) - future → "Prep for Meeting", past → "Discovery Done"
       - booked No Show: opens slide-over instead of auto-orbiting (Fix #5)
     - **`actionItems` useMemo** - Filters terminal stages, non-actionable, applies actionFilter, sorts by sortScore then temperature
     - **`ACTION_GUIDANCE` mapping** - Per-action-label guidance text shown in slide-over banner
     - **`handleActionExecute()`** - Routes bump/stage_change/open_detail, passes guidance to openSlideOver
     - **`openSlideOver()` updated** to accept optional guidance parameter:
       ```typescript
       const openSlideOver = (opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity, guidance?: { label: string; hint: string }) => {
       ```
     - **`renderActionsTab()`** - Full UI with filter pills, section header (violet), table with columns: Name, Stage (w-[140px], whitespace-nowrap, plain span not Badge), Bucket, Next Action (w-[260px] with hint subtitle), Timing (context-aware), Temp, Owner, Execute (primary button + quick alt + dropdown)
     - **Slide-over guidance banner** - Teal banner between BAMFAM warning and content, dismissible with X
     - **`populateForm` updated** to include `next_meeting_at`
   
   - **`lib/salesPipelineService.ts`** (read only, not modified)
     - Contains `SalesPipelineOpportunity` interface, `CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData`, `SalesPipelineService` class
     - `update()` method accepts `next_meeting_at` in its updates parameter
   
   - **`lib/crmService.ts`** (read only)
     - Contains base `CRMOpportunity` interface
   
   - **`components/ui/badge.tsx`** (read only)
     - Default variant has `hover:bg-primary/80` - reason stage badges were changed to plain spans
   
   - **`docs/HoloHive_CRM_Demo (1).html`** (read only)
     - Referenced for action design inspiration, `getNextAction` pattern

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Redundant `getNextAction` call in useMemo**: The urgent filter was calling `getNextAction(opp)` again instead of using the already-mapped `action`. Fixed by changing `({ opp }) =>` to `({ opp, action }) =>`.
   - **Stage badge hover effect**: `Badge` component's default variant includes hover styles. Fixed by replacing with plain `<span>` using same classes.

     5. "No Show" auto-orbited — changed to open_detail

   - **`ActionType` naming conflict with imported `ActivityType`**: Renamed local type to `ActionExecutionType` to avoid confusion.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 16:41:46Z)**_

Now for your two questions:

**1. About the meeting flow:** The `next_meeting_at` field is a single field that gets updated each time. It's not a log of past meetings — it represents the *next upcoming* meeting. So the flow is:
- Book a meeting → set `next_meeting_at`
- Meeting happens (date passes) → the system detects it's in the past → action says "Discovery Done" or similar
- After advancing, BAMFAM logic kicks in: "No future meeting set → Book Next Meeting!"
- User sets a new `next_meeting_at` → cycle continues

So yes, after each meeting finishes and you advance the stage, you'd set the next one.

**2. UI consistency:** Right now it's a plain `datetime-local` input. Looking at the form, all other fields use either `Select` dropdowns, `Input` text fields, or `Textarea` — all with the `auth-input` class. The `datetime-local` input already uses `auth-input` but the native date picker can look different. Let me put it inside a 2-column grid like the other paired fields (Deal Value / Currency pattern), pairing it with a meeting type select since the schema also has `next_meeting_type`.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 17:21:01Z)**_

Here's the difference:

**`next_meeting_at` (Edit form field)** — A **scheduled future date/time** stored on the opportunity itself. It's used by:
- BAMFAM detection ("no future meeting set")
- Actions tab logic (determines "Prep for Meeting" vs "Discovery Done" vs "Schedule Meeting")
- Timing column ("Meeting in 2d")
- The view panel's "Next Meeting" display

**Activity timeline "Meeting"** — A **log entry** recording that a meeting happened (or was scheduled). It's just a note with a type, title, description, outcome, and next step. It doesn't update `next_meeting_at` or affect any pipeline logic.

They serve different purposes — one drives pipeline automation, the other is a history log. But they're disconnected right now: logging a "Meeting" activity doesn't set `next_meeting_at`, and setting `next_meeting_at` doesn't create an activity log.

Would you like me to connect them — e.g., when a user logs a "Meeting" activity, prompt them to also update the next meeting date? Or auto-create a meeting activity when `next_meeting_at` is set?

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-02-24 17:23:02Z)**_

Looking at the guidance hints, only a few actions **tell** users to log activities, but none actually **make** them do it:

- **"Follow Up"** — hint says *"log it as an activity below so the team knows"*
- **"Follow Up Proposal"** — hint says *"Log their response as an activity below"*
- **"No Show"** — hint says *"Log a 'No Show' activity below"*

But these all just open the slide-over and show the hint as a banner. The user can completely ignore it — there's no enforcement or auto-logging. Every other action either bumps, changes a stage, or just opens the detail panel.

So no, there's nothing right now that actually creates an activity log entry from the Actions tab. It's all manual and optional.

```tsx
    placeholder="Description (optional)"
    value={activityForm.description || ''}
    onChange={e => setActivityForm(f => ({ ...f, description: e.target.value }))}
    className="text-sm min-h-[60px] auth-input"
    rows={2}
  />

  {/* Outcome + Next step row */}
  <div className="flex gap-2">
    <Input
      placeholder="Outcome"
      value={activityForm.outcome || ''}
      onChange={e => setActivityForm(f => ({ ...f, outcome: e.target.value }))}
      className="h-9 text-sm flex-1 auth-input"
    />
    <Input
      placeholder="Next step"
      value={activityForm.next_step || ''}
      onChange={e => setActivityForm(f => ({ ...f, next_step: e.target.value }))}
      className="h-9 text-sm flex-1 auth-input"
    />
  </div>

  {/* Next step date + Submit button row */}
  <div className="flex gap-2 items-center">
    <Popover>
      <PopoverTrigger asChild>
        <Button
          variant="outline"
          className="h-9 text-sm flex-1 auth-input justify-start font-normal"
          style={{
            borderColor: '#e5e7eb',
            backgroundColor: 'white',
            color: activityForm.next_step_date ? '#111827' : '#9ca3af'
          }}
        >
          <Calendar className="mr-2 h-4 w-4" />
          {activityForm.next_step_date
            ? new Date(activityForm.next_step_date).toLocaleDateString()
            : 'Next step date'}
        </Button>
      </PopoverTrigger>
      <PopoverContent className="w-auto p-0" align="start">
        <CalendarPicker
          mode="single"
          selected={activityForm.next_step_date ? new Date(activityForm.next_step_date) : undefined}
          onSelect={(date) => setActivityForm(f => ({ ...f, next_step_date: date ? date.toISOString() : undefined }))}
          initialFocus
          classNames={{
            day_selected: 'text-white hover:text-white focus:text-white',
          }}
          modifiersStyles={{
            selected: { backgroundColor: '#3e8692' }
          }}
        />
      </PopoverContent>
    </Popover>
    <Button
      size="sm"
      className="h-9 text-sm hover:opacity-90"
      style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
      onClick={handleAddActivity}
      disabled={isActivitySubmitting || !activityForm.title.trim()}
    >
      {isActivitySubmitting ? '...' : 'Add'}
    </Button>
  </div>
</div>
```

**Form Fields:**
- `type` (ActivityType): 'note', 'call', 'message', 'meeting', 'proposal'
- `title` (string): Required
- `description` (string): Optional
- `outcome` (string): Optional
- `next_step` (string): Optional
- `next_step_date` (ISO string): Optional

---

## 2. **handleAddActivity Function (Lines 527-542)**

```tsx
const handleAddActivity = async () => {
  if (!activityForm.title.trim() || !slideOverOpp) return;
  setIsActivitySubmitting(true);
  try {
    await SalesPipelineService.createActivity({
      ...activityForm,
      opportunity_id: slideOverOpp.id,
    });
    // Reset form after successful submission
    setActivityForm({ opportunity_id: '', type: 'note', title: '' });
    await fetchActivities(slideOverOpp.id);
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error adding activity:', err);
  } finally {
    setIsActivitySubmitting(false);
  }
};
```

**What it does:**
1. Validates that title is not empty and slideOverOpp is set
2. Sets loading state `isActivitySubmitting` to true
3. Calls `SalesPipelineService.createActivity()` with the form data + opportunity_id
4. Resets form to default: `{ opportunity_id: '', type: 'note', title: '' }`
5. Refetches activities from the server
6. Handles errors and sets loading state back to false

---

## 3. **handleActionExecute Function (Lines 882-896)**

```tsx
const handleActionExecute = async (oppId: string, action: ReturnType<typeof getNextAction>, opp: SalesPipelineOpportunity) => {
  setExecutingAction(oppId);
  try {
    if (action.actionType === 'bump') {
      // Execute bump action
      await handleRecordBump(oppId);
    } else if (action.actionType === 'stage_change' && action.targetStage) {
      // Execute stage change action
      await handleStageChange(oppId, action.targetStage, opp.stage);
    } else {
      // action.actionType === 'open_detail'
      // Open slide-over with contextual guidance
      const guidance = ACTION_GUIDANCE[action.label];
      openSlideOver(opp, guidance);
    }
  } finally {
    setExecutingAction(null);
  }
};
```

**What it does by actionType:**
- **`bump`**: Calls `handleRecordBump()` to record a bump
- **`stage_change`**: Calls `handleStageChange()` with the target stage (may trigger orbit/closed-lost prompts)
- **`open_detail`**: Opens the slide-over with contextual guidance text from `ACTION_GUIDANCE` lookup table

---

## 4. **handleRecordBump Function (Lines 487-507)**

```tsx
const handleRecordBump = async (oppId: string) => {
  if (isBumping) return;
  setIsBumping(true);
  
  // Optimistic update — immediately reflect in UI
  setOpportunities(prev => prev.map(o =>
    o.id === oppId ? { ...o, bump_number: Math.min(o.bump_number + 1, 4), last_bump_date: new Date().toISOString() } : o
  ));
  
  try {
    // Call service to persist bump to database
    await SalesPipelineService.recordBump(oppId);
    await fetchData();
    if (activeTab === 'outreach') await fetchOutreach();
    if (slideOverOpp?.id === oppId) {
      await fetchActivities(oppId);
    }
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error recording bump:', err);
    await fetchData(); // revert on error
  } finally {
    setIsBumping(false);
  }
};
```

**What it does:**
1. Guards against multiple bumps in flight with `isBumping` flag
2. **Optimistically updates UI** immediately: increments `bump_number` (capped at 4) and sets `last_bump_date`
3. Calls `SalesPipelineService.recordBump(oppId)` to persist to database
4. Refetches full opportunity list
5. If on outreach tab, refetches outreach data
6. If the slide-over is open for this opportunity, refetches its activities
7. On error, reverts by refetching full data

---

## 5. **handleStageChange Function (Lines 427-443)**

```tsx
const handleStageChange = async (oppId: string, newStage: SalesPipelineStage, currentStage: string) => {
  // These stages require a confirmation dialog before changing
  if (newStage === 'orbit') {
    setOrbitPrompt({ oppId, fromStage: currentStage });
    return;
  }
  if (newStage === 'v2_closed_lost') {
    setClosedLostPrompt({ oppId, fromStage: currentStage });
    return;
  }
  
  // All other stage changes are direct
  try {
    await SalesPipelineService.update(oppId, { stage: newStage as OpportunityStage });
    await fetchData();
    if (activeTab === 'outreach') await fetchOutreach();
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error changing stage:', err);
  }
};
```

**What it does:**
1. If moving to `'orbit'` → opens orbit reason prompt dialog
2. If moving to `'v2_closed_lost'` → opens closed-lost reason prompt dialog
3. Otherwise → directly updates the stage on the server
4. Refetches full opportunity list
5. If on outreach tab, refetches outreach data

## 7. **Dialog/Modal Patterns - orbitPrompt & closedLostPrompt (Lines 3046-3087)**

### Orbit Prompt Dialog:
```tsx
const [orbitPrompt, setOrbitPrompt] = useState<{ oppId: string; fromStage: string } | null>(null);
const [orbitReasonValue, setOrbitReasonValue] = useState<OrbitReason>('no_response');

const confirmOrbit = async () => {
  if (!orbitPrompt) return;
  try {
    await SalesPipelineService.update(orbitPrompt.oppId, {
      stage: 'orbit' as OpportunityStage,
      orbit_reason: orbitReasonValue,
    });
    setOrbitPrompt(null);
    setOrbitReasonValue('no_response');
    await fetchData();
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error moving to orbit:', err);
  }
};

const renderOrbitPrompt = () => (
  <Dialog open={!!orbitPrompt} onOpenChange={open => { if (!open) setOrbitPrompt(null); }}>
    <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-sm z-[80]">
      <DialogHeader>
        <DialogTitle>Move to Orbit</DialogTitle>
        <DialogDescription>Select the reason for moving this opportunity to orbit.</DialogDescription>
      </DialogHeader>
      <Select value={orbitReasonValue} onValueChange={v => setOrbitReasonValue(v as OrbitReason)}>
        <SelectTrigger><SelectValue /></SelectTrigger>
        <SelectContent>
          {ORBIT_REASONS.map(r => (
            <SelectItem key={r.value} value={r.value}>{r.label}</SelectItem>
          ))}
        </SelectContent>
      </Select>
      <DialogFooter>
        <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setOrbitPrompt(null)}>Cancel</Button>
        <Button onClick={confirmOrbit} style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692' }}>Confirm</Button>
      </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
  </Dialog>
);
```

### Closed Lost Prompt Dialog:
```tsx
const [closedLostPrompt, setClosedLostPrompt] = useState<{ oppId: string; fromStage: string } | null>(null);
const [closedLostReasonValue, setClosedLostReasonValue] = useState('');

const confirmClosedLost = async () => {
  if (!closedLostPrompt) return;
  try {
    await SalesPipelineService.update(closedLostPrompt.oppId, {
      stage: 'v2_closed_lost' as OpportunityStage,
      closed_lost_reason: closedLostReasonValue || undefined,
    });
    setClosedLostPrompt(null);
    setClosedLostReasonValue('');
    await fetchData();
  } catch (err) {
    console.error('Error closing lost:', err);
  }
};

const renderClosedLostPrompt = () => (
  <Dialog open={!!closedLostPrompt} onOpenChange={open => { if (!open) setClosedLostPrompt(null); }}>
    <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-sm z-[80]">
      <DialogHeader>
        <DialogTitle>Close as Lost</DialogTitle>
        <DialogDescription>Optionally add a reason for closing this opportunity.</DialogDescription>
      </DialogHeader>
      <Input
        value={closedLostReasonValue}
        onChange={e => setClosedLostReasonValue(e.target.value)}
        placeholder="Reason (optional)"
      />
      <DialogFooter>
        <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setClosedLostPrompt(null)}>Cancel</Button>
        <Button onClick={confirmClosedLost} variant="destructive">Close Lost</Button>
      </DialogFooter>
    </DialogContent>
  </Dialog>
);
```

const ACTION_GUIDANCE: Record<string, { label: string; hint: string }> = {
  'Book Next Meeting!': { label: 'Book Next Meeting', hint: 'Click the pencil icon (Edit) → set the Next Meeting date field. No deal should exist without a future meeting (BAMFAM).' },
  'Book Meeting': { label: 'Book Meeting', hint: 'Click the pencil icon (Edit) → set the Next Meeting date field to stay BAMFAM-compliant.' },
  'Get TG Handle': { label: 'Get TG Handle', hint: 'DM them asking for their Telegram handle, then click Edit → fill in the TG Handle field. Once entered, click "Got TG!" to advance.' },
  'Follow Up': { label: 'Follow Up', hint: 'Send a follow-up DM. After messaging, log it as an activity below so the team knows.' },
  'Schedule Meeting': { label: 'Schedule Meeting', hint: 'Send Calendly or propose a time. Click the pencil icon (Edit) → set the Next Meeting date field once confirmed.' },
  'Prep for Meeting': { label: 'Prep for Meeting', hint: 'Review their notes, bucket, and past activity below. Update Notes with talking points before the call.' },
  'Follow Up Proposal': { label: 'Follow Up Proposal', hint: 'Message them to check if they reviewed the proposal. Log their response as an activity below.' },
  'Need More Info': { label: 'Need More Info', hint: 'They need more details before a proposal. Add notes on what they need, then follow up.' },
  'Resurrect': { label: 'Resurrect Check', hint: 'It\'s been 90+ days. Review their notes — DM to re-engage, or move to Lost if no longer viable.' },
  'Chase Signature': { label: 'Chase Signature', hint: 'Follow up on the contract. Once they sign, use the "Signed!" button to close the deal.' },
  'Reschedule': { label: 'Reschedule Meeting', hint: 'Meeting needs rescheduling. Click the pencil icon (Edit) → update the Next Meeting date field.' },
  'No Show': { label: 'No Show', hint: 'They didn\'t show up. Log a "No Show" activity below and decide whether to reschedule or orbit.' },
  'Keep Bumping': { label: 'Keep Bumping', hint: 'Override the orbit suggestion — review notes and continue follow-up DMs.' },
};

## Summary

This is a comprehensive activity logging and action handling system:

1. **Activity Form** captures rich context (type, title, description, outcome, next steps, follow-up dates)
2. **handleAddActivity** validates, submits, resets, and refetches
3. **handleActionExecute** routes 3 action types: bump, stage_change, or open_detail (with guidance)
4. **handleRecordBump** uses optimistic UI + server persistence + conditional refetches
5. **handleStageChange** routes 'orbit' and 'v2_closed_lost' to confirmation dialogs, others directly to server
6. **Dialog patterns** (orbitPrompt, closedLostPrompt) gather required metadata before state transitions
7. **ACTION_GUIDANCE** provides contextual help text for manual actions

```text
   151→// ============================================
   152→// Sales Pipeline Service

   154→
   155→export class SalesPipelineService {
   156→  // ----------------------------------------
   157→  // OPPORTUNITIES
   158→  // ----------------------------------------
   159→
   160→  static async getAll(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]> {
   161→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   162→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   163→      .select(`
   164→        *,
   165→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   166→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   167→      `)
   168→      .in('stage', ALL_V2_STAGES)
   169→      .order('position', { ascending: true })
   170→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   171→
   172→    if (error) {
   173→      console.error('Error fetching sales pipeline opportunities:', error);
   174→      throw error;
   175→    }
   176→    return (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[];
   177→  }
   178→
   179→  static async create(data: CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   180→    // Dedup check: name + tg_handle
   181→    if (data.name && data.tg_handle) {
   182→      const dedupKey = `${data.name.toLowerCase().trim()}::${data.tg_handle.toLowerCase().trim()}`;
   183→      const { data: existing } = await supabase
   184→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   185→        .select('id')
   186→        .eq('dedup_key', dedupKey)
   187→        .maybeSingle();
   188→
   189→      if (existing) {
   190→        throw new Error('A deal with this name and TG handle already exists');
   191→      }
   192→
   193→      (data as any).dedup_key = dedupKey;
   194→    }
   195→
   196→    const insertData = {
   197→      ...data,
   198→      stage: data.stage || 'cold_dm',
   199→    };
   200→
   201→    const { data: created, error } = await supabase
   202→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   203→      .insert([insertData])
   204→      .select()
   205→      .single();
   206→
   207→    if (error) throw error;
   208→
   209→    // Record stage history
   210→    await CRMService.recordStageHistory('opportunity', created.id, null, insertData.stage || 'cold_dm');
   211→
   212→    // Auto-calculate temperature score
   213→    await this.recalcTemperature(created.id);
   214→
   215→    return created as SalesPipelineOpportunity;
   216→  }
   217→
   218→  static async update(
   219→    id: string,
   220→    updates: Partial<CreateSalesPipelineOpportunityData> & {
   221→      warm_sub_state?: WarmSubState | null;
   222→      orbit_reason?: OrbitReason | null;
   223→      closed_lost_reason?: string | null;
   224→      next_meeting_at?: string | null;
   225→      next_meeting_type?: string | null;
   226→      bump_number?: number;
   227→      last_bump_date?: string | null;
   228→      bucket?: Bucket | null;
   229→      calendly_sent_via?: CalendlySentVia;
   230→      calendly_sent_date?: string | null;
   231→      calendly_booked_date?: string | null;
   232→      gc_opened?: string;
   233→    }
   234→  ): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   235→    // Get current state for stage history
   236→    const { data: current } = await supabase
   237→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   238→      .select('*')
   239→      .eq('id', id)
   240→      .single();
   241→
   242→    const updateData: any = { ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   243→
   244→    // Auto-set timestamps on stage transitions
   245→    if (updates.stage && current) {
   246→      if (updates.stage === 'discovery_done' && !current.discovery_call_at) {
   247→        updateData.discovery_call_at = new Date().toISOString();
   248→      }
   249→      if (updates.stage === 'proposal_sent' && !current.proposal_sent_at) {
   250→        updateData.proposal_sent_at = new Date().toISOString();
   251→      }
   252→      if ((updates.stage === 'v2_closed_won' || updates.stage === 'v2_closed_lost') && !current.closed_at) {
   253→        updateData.closed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   254→      }
   255→    }
   256→
   257→    // Auto-set bucket_changed_at
   258→    if (updates.bucket && current && updates.bucket !== current.bucket) {
   259→      updateData.bucket_changed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   260→    }
   261→
   262→    const { data: updated, error } = await supabase
   263→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   264→      .update(updateData)
   265→      .eq('id', id)
   266→      .select()
   267→      .single();
   268→
   269→    if (error) throw error;
   270→
   271→    // Record stage change
   272→    if (updates.stage && current && current.stage !== updates.stage) {
   273→      await CRMService.recordStageHistory('opportunity', id, current.stage, updates.stage);
   274→    }
   275→
   276→    // Recalculate temperature score after stage/bucket/key field changes
   277→    await this.recalcTemperature(id);
   278→
   279→    return updated as SalesPipelineOpportunity;
   280→  }
   281→
   282→  static async delete(id: string): Promise<void> {
   283→    const { error } = await supabase
   284→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   285→      .delete()
   286→      .eq('id', id);
   287→
   288→    if (error) throw error;
   289→  }
   290→
   291→  // ----------------------------------------
   292→  // BUMPS
   293→  // ----------------------------------------
   294→
   295→  static async recordBump(id: string): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   296→    const { data: current } = await supabase
   297→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   298→      .select('bump_number')
   299→      .eq('id', id)
   300→      .single();
   301→
   302→    const newBumpNumber = (current?.bump_number || 0) + 1;
   303→
   304→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   305→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   306→      .update({
   307→        bump_number: newBumpNumber,
   308→        last_bump_date: new Date().toISOString(),
   309→        last_contacted_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   310→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   311→      })
   312→      .eq('id', id)
   313→      .select()
   314→      .single();
   315→
   316→    if (error) throw error;
   317→
   318→    // Also create a bump activity
   319→    await this.createActivity({
   320→      opportunity_id: id,
   321→      type: 'bump',
   322→      title: `Bump #${newBumpNumber}`,
   323→    });
   324→
   325→    // Recalculate temperature score
   326→    await this.recalcTemperature(id);
   327→
   328→    return data as SalesPipelineOpportunity;
   329→  }
   330→
   331→  static async reduceBump(id: string): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity> {
   332→    const { data: current } = await supabase
   333→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   334→      .select('bump_number')
   335→      .eq('id', id)
   336→      .single();
   337→
   338→    const newBumpNumber = Math.max((current?.bump_number || 0) - 1, 0);
   339→
   340→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   341→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   342→      .update({
   343→        bump_number: newBumpNumber,
   344→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   345→      })
   346→      .eq('id', id)
   347→      .select()
   348→      .single();
   349→
   350→    if (error) throw error;
   351→    return data as SalesPipelineOpportunity;
   352→  }
   353→
   354→  // ----------------------------------------
   355→  // ACTIVITIES
   356→  // ----------------------------------------
   357→
   358→  static async getActivities(opportunityId: string): Promise<CRMActivity[]> {
   359→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   360→      .from('crm_activities')
   361→      .select('*')
   362→      .eq('opportunity_id', opportunityId)
   363→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   364→
   365→    if (error) throw error;
   366→    return data || [];
   367→  }
   368→
   369→  static async createActivity(activityData: CreateActivityData): Promise<CRMActivity> {
   370→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
   371→
   372→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   373→      .from('crm_activities')
   374→      .insert([{
   375→        ...activityData,
   376→        owner_id: user?.id || null,
   377→      }])
   378→      .select()
   379→      .single();
   380→
   381→    if (error) throw error;
   382→
   383→    // Recalculate temperature score (new activity affects engagement)
   384→    await this.recalcTemperature(activityData.opportunity_id);
   385→
   386→    return data;
   387→  }
   388→
   389→  // ----------------------------------------
   390→  // BAMFAM VIOLATIONS
   391→  // ----------------------------------------
   392→
   393→  static async getBAMFAMViolations(): Promise<SalesPipelineOpportunity[]> {
   394→    // BAMFAM: past discovery_done with no next_meeting or overdue next_meeting
   395→    const postDiscoveryStages: SalesPipelineStage[] = [
   396→      'discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract',
   397→    ];
   398→
   399→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   400→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   401→      .select(`
   402→        *,
   403→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   404→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   405→      `)
   406→      .in('stage', postDiscoveryStages)
   407→      .or('next_meeting_at.is.null,next_meeting_at.lt.' + new Date().toISOString());
   408→
   409→    if (error) throw error;
   410→    return (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[];
   411→  }
   412→
   413→  // ----------------------------------------
   414→  // STAGES HELPER
   415→  // ----------------------------------------
   416→
   417→  static getAvailableStages(dmAccount: DmAccount): SalesPipelineStage[] {
   418→    if (dmAccount === 'closer') return PATH_A_STAGES;
   419→    return PATH_B_STAGES;
   420→  }
   421→
   422→  // ----------------------------------------
   423→  // METRICS
   424→  // ----------------------------------------
   425→
   426→  static async getMetrics(): Promise<{
   427→    totalCount: number;
   428→    bucketA: number;
   429→    bucketB: number;
   430→    bucketC: number;
   431→    activeValue: number;
   432→    bamfamViolations: number;
   433→  }> {
   434→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   435→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   436→      .select('stage, bucket, deal_value, next_meeting_at')
   437→      .in('stage', ALL_V2_STAGES);
   438→
   439→    if (error) throw error;
   440→
   441→    const opps = data || [];
   442→    const activeStages = PIPELINE_STAGES;
   443→    const active = opps.filter(o => activeStages.includes(o.stage as SalesPipelineStage));
   444→
   445→    const postDiscovery = ['discovery_done', 'proposal_sent', 'proposal_call', 'v2_contract'];
   446→    const now = new Date().toISOString();
   447→    const bamfam = opps.filter(o =>
   448→      postDiscovery.includes(o.stage) &&
   449→      (!o.next_meeting_at || o.next_meeting_at < now)
   450→    );
   451→
   452→    return {
   453→      totalCount: opps.length,
   454→      bucketA: opps.filter(o => o.bucket === 'A').length,
   455→      bucketB: opps.filter(o => o.bucket === 'B').length,
   456→      bucketC: opps.filter(o => o.bucket === 'C').length,
   457→      activeValue: active.reduce((sum, o) => sum + (o.deal_value || 0), 0),
   458→      bamfamViolations: bamfam.length,
   459→    };
   460→  }
   461→
   462→  // ----------------------------------------
   463→  // POSITION UPDATES
   464→  // ----------------------------------------
   465→
   466→  static async updatePositions(positions: { id: string; position: number; stage?: OpportunityStage }[]): Promise<void> {
   467→    const updates = positions.map(({ id, position, stage }) => {
   468→      const updateData: any = { position, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   469→      if (stage) updateData.stage = stage;
   470→      return supabase
   471→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   472→        .update(updateData)
   473→        .eq('id', id);
   474→    });
   475→
   476→    const results = await Promise.all(updates);
   477→    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   478→    if (errors.length > 0) {
   479→      console.error('Errors updating positions:', errors);
   480→      throw errors[0].error;
   481→    }
   482→  }
   483→
   484→  // ----------------------------------------
   485→  // COLD DM PAGINATED (Outreach tab)
   486→  // ----------------------------------------
   487→
   488→  static async getColdDmsPaginated(
   489→    page: number,
   490→    pageSize: number,
   491→    filters: {
   492→      dm_account?: DmAccount;
   493→      bucket?: Bucket;
   494→      bumpRange?: 'none' | '1-2' | '3+';
   495→      searchTerm?: string;
   496→      sortBy?: string;
   497→      sortAsc?: boolean;
   498→      owner_id?: string;
   499→    }
   500→  ): Promise<{ data: SalesPipelineOpportunity[]; count: number }> {
   501→    const from = (page - 1) * pageSize;
   502→    const to = from + pageSize - 1;
   503→
   504→    let query = supabase
   505→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   506→      .select(`
   507→        *,
   508→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   509→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   510→      `, { count: 'exact' })
   511→      .eq('stage', 'cold_dm');
   512→
   513→    // Optional filters
   514→    if (filters.dm_account) {
   515→      query = query.eq('dm_account', filters.dm_account);
   516→    }
   517→    if (filters.bucket) {
   518→      query = query.eq('bucket', filters.bucket);
   519→    }
   520→    if (filters.searchTerm) {
   521→      query = query.ilike('name', `%${filters.searchTerm}%`);
   522→    }
   523→    if (filters.owner_id) {
   524→      query = query.eq('owner_id', filters.owner_id);
   525→    }
   526→    if (filters.bumpRange === 'none') {
   527→      query = query.eq('bump_number', 0);
   528→    } else if (filters.bumpRange === '1-2') {
   529→      query = query.gte('bump_number', 1).lte('bump_number', 2);
   530→    } else if (filters.bumpRange === '3+') {
   531→      query = query.gte('bump_number', 3);
   532→    }
   533→
   534→    // Sorting
   535→    const sortField = filters.sortBy || 'created_at';
   536→    const ascending = filters.sortAsc ?? false;
   537→    query = query.order(sortField, { ascending });
   538→
   539→    // Pagination
   540→    query = query.range(from, to);
   541→
   542→    const { data, error, count } = await query;
   543→
   544→    if (error) {
   545→      console.error('Error fetching cold DMs paginated:', error);
   546→      throw error;
   547→    }
   548→
   549→    return { data: (data || []) as SalesPipelineOpportunity[], count: count || 0 };
   550→  }
   551→
   552→  // ----------------------------------------
   553→  // BULK OPERATIONS
   554→  // ----------------------------------------
   555→
   556→  // ----------------------------------------
   557→  // TEMPERATURE SCORE
   558→  // ----------------------------------------
   559→
   560→  static async recalcTemperature(id: string): Promise<number | null> {
   561→    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('calculate_temperature_score', { opp_id: id });
   562→    if (error) {
   563→      console.error('Error recalculating temperature:', error);
   564→      return null;
   565→    }
   566→    return data;
   567→  }
   568→
   569→  static async recalcAllTemperatures(): Promise<number | null> {
   570→    const { data, error } = await supabase.rpc('recalculate_all_temperature_scores');
   571→    if (error) {
   572→      console.error('Error recalculating all temperatures:', error);
   573→      return null;
   574→    }
   575→    return data;
   576→  }
   577→
   578→  // ----------------------------------------
   579→  // BULK OPERATIONS
   580→  // ----------------------------------------
   581→
   582→  static async bulkRecordBump(ids: string[]): Promise<void> {
   583→    await Promise.all(ids.map(id => this.recordBump(id)));
   584→  }
   585→
   586→  static async bulkUpdateStage(ids: string[], stage: SalesPipelineStage): Promise<void> {
   587→    const now = new Date().toISOString();
   588→    const updates = ids.map(id =>
   589→      supabase
   590→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   591→        .update({ stage, updated_at: now })
   592→        .eq('id', id)
   593→    );
   594→
   595→    const results = await Promise.all(updates);
   596→    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   597→    if (errors.length > 0) {
   598→      console.error('Errors in bulk stage update:', errors);
   599→      throw errors[0].error;
   600→    }
   601→  }
   602→
   603→  static async bulkDelete(ids: string[]): Promise<void> {
   604→    const deletes = ids.map(id =>
   605→      supabase
   606→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   607→        .delete()
   608→        .eq('id', id)
   609→    );
   610→
   611→    const results = await Promise.all(deletes);
   612→    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   613→    if (errors.length > 0) {
   614→      console.error('Errors in bulk delete:', errors);
   615→      throw errors[0].error;
   616→    }
   617→  }
   618→}
```

```text
  2785→                        <div className="flex items-center gap-1 mt-1 text-sm text-blue-600">
  2786→                          <ArrowRight className="h-3 w-3 flex-shrink-0" />
  2787→                          <span>{act.next_step}</span>
  2788→                          {act.next_step_date && (
  2789→                            <span className="text-gray-400 ml-1">
  2790→                              ({format(new Date(act.next_step_date), 'MMM d')})
  2791→                            </span>
  2792→                          )}
  2793→                        </div>
  2794→                      )}
  2795→                      <span className="text-xs text-gray-400 mt-1.5 block">
  2796→                        {formatDistanceToNow(new Date(act.created_at), { addSuffix: true })}
  2797→                      </span>
  2798→                    </div>
  2799→                  </div>
  2800→                ))}
  2801→              </div>
  2802→            </div>

  2812→  // ============================================

  2815→
  2816→  const renderFormDialog = () => {
  2817→    const isEdit = !!editingOpp;

  2819→    const isOpen = isCreateOpen || (!!editingOpp && slideOverMode !== 'edit');
  2820→
  2821→    return (
  2822→      <Dialog open={isOpen} onOpenChange={open => {
  2823→        if (!open) { setIsCreateOpen(false); setEditingOpp(null); setForm({ name: '' }); }
  2824→      }}>
  2825→        <DialogContent className="max-w-md">
  2826→          <DialogHeader>
  2827→            <DialogTitle>{isEdit ? 'Edit Opportunity' : 'New Opportunity'}</DialogTitle>
  2828→            <DialogDescription>
  2829→              {isEdit ? 'Update opportunity details.' : 'Add a new opportunity to the sales pipeline.'}
  2830→            </DialogDescription>
  2831→          </DialogHeader>
  2832→          <form onSubmit={e => { e.preventDefault(); isEdit ? handleUpdate() : handleCreate(); }}>
  2833→            <div className="grid gap-4 py-4">
  2834→              {/* Basic Info */}
  2835→              <div className="grid gap-2">
  2836→                <Label>Name *</Label>
  2837→                <Input
  2838→                  value={form.name}
  2839→                  onChange={e => setForm(f => ({ ...f, name: e.target.value }))}
  2840→                  placeholder="Company or contact name"
  2841→                  className="auth-input"
  2842→                />
  2843→              </div>
  2844→
  2845→              <div className="grid grid-cols-2 gap-4">
  2846→                <div className="grid gap-2">
  2847→                  <Label>TG Handle</Label>
  2848→                  <Input
  2849→                    value={form.tg_handle || ''}
  2850→                    onChange={e => setForm(f => ({ ...f, tg_handle: e.target.value }))}
  2851→                    placeholder="@handle"
  2852→                    className="auth-input"
  2853→                  />
  2854→                </div>
  2855→                <div className="grid gap-2">
  2856→                  <Label>Owner</Label>
  2857→                  <Select
  2858→                    value={form.owner_id || ''}
  2859→                    onValueChange={v => setForm(f => ({ ...f, owner_id: v }))}
  2860→                  >
  2861→                    <SelectTrigger className="auth-input"><SelectValue placeholder="Select..." /></SelectTrigger>
  2862→                    <SelectContent>
  2863→                      {users.map(u => (
  2864→                        <SelectItem key={u.id} value={u.id}>{u.name || u.email}</SelectItem>
  2865→                      ))}
  2866→                    </SelectContent>
  2867→                  </Select>
  2868→                </div>
  2869→              </div>
```

```text
    78→  const { setNodeRef, isOver } = useDroppable({ id });
    79→  return (
    80→    <div ref={setNodeRef} className={`${className} ${isOver ? 'ring-2 ring-blue-400 ring-offset-2' : ''}`}>

    82→    </div>

    87→  const { attributes, listeners, setNodeRef, transform, transition, isDragging } = useSortable({ id });
    88→  const style = {
    89→    transform: CSS.Transform.toString(transform),
    90→    transition,
    91→    opacity: isDragging ? 0.5 : 1,
    92→  };
    93→  return (
    94→    <div ref={setNodeRef} style={style} {...attributes} {...listeners}>
    95→      {children}
    96→    </div>
    97→  );
    98→}

   111→      <TableCell className="w-10">
```

## 1. **Activity Form Section in Slide-Over (Lines 2670-2759)**

The activity form is rendered in the slide-over with the following fields:

```tsx
<div className="space-y-3 mb-6 p-4 bg-gray-50 rounded-lg border border-gray-200">

    <Select
      value={activityForm.type}
      onValueChange={v => setActivityForm(f => ({ ...f, type: v as ActivityType }))}

    />

    placeholder="Description (optional)"
    value={activityForm.description || ''}

  />
```
